evidence: Package hierarchy: api → exploration → query → model/utils/config; indexer is an independent leaf. Dependency direction is consistently top-down with no upward references observed.

WordSketchApiServer constructor instantiates SemanticFieldExplorer directly rather than accepting it as a parameter, creating a construction-time coupling that makes the server harder to test in isolation.

SemanticFieldExplorer constructor instantiates CollocateProfileComparator internally; comparator is then exposed via getComparator() accessor called from ExplorationHandlers — creating an indirect dependency path api→exploration→comparator that bypasses the exploration abstraction.
LongIntHashMap in utils package has no import references in any production file observed; appears to be orphaned code that blurs the utils boundary by retaining a presumably unused implementation.
com.sun.net.httpserver is used directly in WordSketchApiServer and HttpApiUtils — a JDK internal API that is accessible but introduces a fragile platform coupling across the api module boundary.
Main.java correctly instantiates subsystems and wires them together at the entry point — appropriate hub role.
No circular package dependencies detected across api/exploration/query/model/config/indexer/utils.

strengths: Clear unidirectional layering: api consumes exploration and query; neither depends upward.
QueryExecutor interface creates a clean boundary between the api/exploration layers and the BlackLab implementation.
Indexer package is properly isolated and only touched by Main.
No circular dependencies detected anywhere in the package graph.
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: The main issues are construction-time coupling (components create collaborators rather than receiving them) and a single orphaned utility class, rather than any structural cycle or layer violation.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The codebase scores well on dependency direction and boundary integrity — the most critical cross-module properties. The deductions come from: in-constructor instantiation of SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator (testability cost, blast-radius concern), the odd getComparator() indirection that leaks a collaborator through its owner, and the orphaned LongIntHashMap. These are module-scope issues, not codebase-wide violations. Score of 77 reflects an architecture that works cleanly but has unfixed construction coupling and one dead utility.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer constructs SemanticFieldExplorer internally, preventing injection and creating hidden coupling
evidence: Line 46: SemanticFieldExplorer semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor); inside constructor — caller cannot substitute a mock or alternative implementation
issues.suggestion: Accept SemanticFieldExplorer as a constructor parameter in WordSketchApiServer; construct it in Main.java alongside the executor, keeping construction at the composition root.

issues.summary: LongIntHashMap in utils is not imported by any production file, blurring the utils boundary
evidence: No import of LongIntHashMap found in any other production source file; class appears to be dead code retained in the utils boundary.
issues.suggestion: Either integrate LongIntHashMap where it adds value (e.g., frequency accumulation in CollocateQueryHelper), or delete it to keep the utils package boundary clean.

issues.summary: CollocateProfileComparator is exposed through SemanticFieldExplorer.getComparator() rather than being directly accessible to the api layer
evidence: ExplorationHandlers calls semanticFieldExplorer.getComparator().compareCollocateProfiles(...) — ExplorationHandlers reaches through SemanticFieldExplorer to get a collaborator, bypassing the intended abstraction boundary.
issues.suggestion: Expose compareCollocateProfiles directly on SemanticFieldExplorer as a delegate method, or inject CollocateProfileComparator separately into ExplorationHandlers.

root_causes: Construction-time wiring rather than dependency injection is the recurring pattern — components build their own collaborators, limiting testability and creating implicit cross-module coupling.
likely_symptoms: Low unit test coverage for WordSketchApiServer and ExplorationHandlers due to tight construction coupling.
